ScriptReference.name: Create and add IO to PI

Comment.value: Called from a button in IO Page item setting. Button only visible for IO Input blocks
Also called when creating a new Page item in a page - that's when we need the page_item_id
This script creates a new IO for "write" and adds it automativcally to the page item. The purpose is to speed up the authoring process.
Usage type can be Member or Group or Session - given by the package type
Standard IO
Set up IO values
Find the Write IOs (can be more than one)

LayoutReference.name: TABLE FAT IO Value @ Page item

Calculation.Text: FAT IO Value @ Page item::IO Block template id

Comment.value: Check that function is Write - only write IOs are created with this function
IO context is given by the Package usage type and already set with Setup IO values
Create only WRITE IOs

Calculation.Text: FAT IO Value @ Page item::IO Type
"What do you want to store in this IO?"

Comment.value: The Hotspot building block (for example) requires an IO but is populated by a page item list. That's why this IO does not need a Title list.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "title_id" ; $title_id ) & SetProperty ( "list_id" ; $list_id ) & SetProperty ( "list_template_id" ; $list_template_id ) & SetProperty ( "io_name" ; Proper($io_name) ) & SetProperty ( "io_type" ; $io_type )

LayoutReference.name: TABLE FAT IO Value @ Page item

Comment.value: Create an IO of the right type
Set a valid object name - no duplicates allowed in same title, not exceeding number of characters

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "source_textfield" ; GetFieldName ( FAT IO::Name ) ) & SetProperty ( "new_name" ; $io_name ) & SetProperty ( "max_characters" ; 32 )

Comment.value: Setting FATversion default values
Setting default write context to package usage type = io_write_context
Setting default permissions to package usage type = io_write_context

Calculation.Text: ($io_type = "Rate" Or $io_type ="Dilemma" Or $io_type ="Select" Or $io_type ="Choice" Or $io_type ="IO group") And $list_id≠""

Comment.value: These IO types have a list - which enables the Insights function automatically
If there are modifiers connected
If there are fields connected - create one field!

Calculation.Text: Case ( PatternCount (FAT Title::Title Backend; "xAPI") And IO Type::xAPI enabled ; 1 ; 0 )

Comment.value: Connect the IO to this block

LayoutReference.name: TABLE FAT IO Value @ Page item

ScriptReference.name: Modify IO Value at Page item

Calculation.Text: "Something is wrong with auto creation of IOs"

Comment.value: Check if we created any IOs

Calculation.Text: "Check the IO pop-up meny to select an existing IO or go to Content-> IOs to create one manually."

"A new IO of type " & Upper($io_type) & " and with the name \"" & $io_name & "\" created and connected to this page item." & ¶ & ¶ & "This IO is now available everywhere in the Title. If you want to modify the IO - go to Content -> IOs."
"Several new IOs were created and connected to this page item." & ¶ & ¶ & "These IOs are now available everywhere in the Title. If you want to modify the IOs - go to Content -> IOs."
BlockTemplate @ Page item::PrimaryKey ≠ "9D44EDFB-8DE5-42DA-B191-A08F8E6B7D78" //Categorize V2 is a write enabled block but no new IOs should be created.
Case ( IsEmpty(TF PI Text 1::Text); "IO for " & FAT Block @ Page item::Name; TF PI Text 1::Text)
"What do you want to store in this IO?"

SetProperty ( "title_id" ; $title_id ) & SetProperty ( "list_id" ; $list_id ) & SetProperty ( "list_template_id" ; $list_template_id ) & SetProperty ( "io_name" ; Proper($io_name) ) & SetProperty ( "io_type" ; $io_type )

Comment.value: These IO types have a list - which enables the Insights function automatically

Calculation.Text: "A new IO of type " & Upper($io_type) & " and with the name \"" & $io_base_name & "\" created and connected to this page item." & ¶ & ¶ & "This IO is now available everywhere in the Title. If you want to modify the IO - go to Content -> IOs."

Comment.value: Not a write IO
Current group members is the most commonly used context for reading IOs
LMS or xAPI
Search for stateToggle "io_context"
